source_title: Geospatial Python - Full Course for Beginners with Geopandas

concepts.name: GeoPandas
concepts.definition: An open-source library that extends Pandas by adding support for geospatial data, allowing spatial operations on geometric types.

concepts.name: GeoDataFrame
concepts.definition: A tabular data structure that contains a 'geometry' column in addition to standard pandas data columns.

concepts.name: GeoSeries
concepts.definition: A vector where each element is a set of shapes corresponding to one observation (the geometry column of a GeoDataFrame).

concepts.name: Coordinate Reference System (CRS)
concepts.definition: A framework used to precisely measure locations on the surface of the Earth as coordinates.

concepts.name: Geometry Attributes
concepts.definition: Spatial properties associated with geographic data such as area, centroid, and boundary bounds.

steps.explanation: Required to execute Geopandas code. Can be done via direct download, Homebrew (Mac), or package managers (Linux).
steps.gotcha: Be aware of the difference between global and virtual environments.

steps.explanation: A notebook service is recommended for interactive geospatial data visualization.
steps.gotcha: Google Colab can be used as a hosted alternative.

steps.explanation: Get notebooks and datasets from modern-gi.xyz for hands-on practice.
steps.gotcha: Ensure you sign up for a free account to access data.

steps.explanation: To understand specific operations available for GeoSeries and GeoDataFrames.
steps.gotcha: The API reference is the most detailed source for finding methods like .area or .bounds.

steps.explanation: To find pre-built snippets for common tasks like mapping the NYC Burrows.

gotchas.description: Confusion between Pandas and GeoPandas data types.
gotchas.context: When performing standard pandas operations on a GeoDataFrame.
gotchas.solution: Remember that a GeoDataFrame is a DataFrame, but it requires a 'geometry' column to support spatial functions.

gotchas.context: Installing packages globally vs in a virtual environment.
gotchas.solution: Use virtual environments or notebook services like Google Colab to avoid library conflicts unique to geospatial dependencies (GDAL, GEOS, etc.).
